title: leucochrysum albicans subsp. albicans
intro: Leucochrysum albicans subsp. albicans, also known as Stink Bush, is a flowering plant native to New South Wales in Australia, characterized by its strong and unpleasant odor. The medicinal properties of Leucochrysum albicans subsp. albicans include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including arthritis, joint pain, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Leucochrysum albicans subsp. albicans include essential oils, capsules, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
